skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Video outpainting on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — extend the spatial canvas of a video, change aspect ratio (9:16 vertical to 16:9 horizontal or vice versa), add environment beyond the original frame while preserving the central action. Routes prompt-shaped spatial extension through Wan 2-7 edit-video and points the agent at dedicated ComfyUI outpaint workflows when seam quality matters for hero delivery. Triggers on "video outpaint", "video outpainting", "extend video canvas", "expand video frame", "uncrop video", "aspect ratio change", "vertical to horizontal video", "16:9 from 9:16", "TikTok to YouTube", or any explicit ask to extend a video spatially beyond its original frame.
skill.summary: Extend video canvases spatially using RunComfy CLI — change aspect ratios, add environment beyond the original frame, and route between Wan 2-7 edit-video and ComfyUI workflows based on quality requirements.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 64 patterns (external commands, hardcoded URLs, filesystem references, alleged weak crypto) and assigned a risk score of 100/100. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 33 external command detections are markdown code blocks documenting standard runcomfy CLI usage — this is the skill's documented purpose. The 22 URL detections point to the RunComfy SaaS platform documentation. The 7 'weak crypto' detections misidentify the AI model name 'Wan 2-7' as a cryptographic algorithm. The filesystem reference at line 148 is transparency documentation about token storage location, not an access attempt. No prompt injection, malicious intent, or data exfiltration detected. The skill is restricted to Bash(runcomfy *) only.

low_findings.title: Documented CLI Commands — False Positive
low_findings.description: 33 backtick-enclosed shell commands detected in markdown code blocks. These are documentation examples showing npm install, runcomfy login, and runcomfy run invocations. The skill's purpose is guiding AI agents to use the runcomfy CLI for video outpainting. Commands are scoped to Bash(runcomfy *) in the skill manifest.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs — False Positive
low_findings.description: 22 URLs detected pointing to runcomfy.com, skills.sh, and docs.runcomfy.com. These are legitimate documentation and service links for the RunComfy SaaS platform. The skill is a RunComfy CLI wrapper, so platform URLs are inherent to its function.

low_findings.title: AI Model Name Misidentified as Weak Cryptography — False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'wan' substring as a weak cryptographic algorithm. 'Wan 2-7' is an AI video generation model name (Wan 2-7 edit-video), not a cryptographic function. No cryptographic code exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Token Storage Path Documented — False Positive
low_findings.description: Line 148 documents that the runcomfy CLI stores authentication tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is security transparency documentation informing users about token storage behavior, not an attempt to access hidden files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Text — False Positive
low_findings.description: The phrase 'Pick for:' and 'Avoid for:' at line 52 is model selection guidance for outpainting workflows, not system reconnaissance. It advises which AI model to use for different quality requirements.

content.user_title: Extend Video Canvas with AI-Powered Outpainting
content.value_statement: Videos shot in one aspect ratio often need to be repurposed for different platforms without reshooting. This skill enables AI agents to extend video canvases spatially using the RunComfy CLI, preserving central action while adding matching environment beyond the original frame.
actual_capabilities: Run Wan 2-7 edit-video model for prompt-shaped spatial canvas extension
Change video aspect ratio from vertical to horizontal and vice versa
Guide AI agents to the appropriate outpainting model for each quality tier
Handle common platform-specific format conversions such as TikTok to YouTube
Route hero-grade production requests to dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows
Document security practices for CLI token storage and shell injection prevention
limitations: Cannot perform image outpainting for still images, only video
Cannot extend videos temporally by adding more frames
Seam quality from Wan 2-7 may not meet hero ad delivery standards
ComfyUI outpainting workflows require GUI interaction and cannot be automated via CLI

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Repurposing
use_cases.description: Social media managers convert vertical TikTok videos to horizontal YouTube format while preserving the central content and background consistency.

use_cases.title: Video Production Pipeline Integration
use_cases.description: Video editors use CLI-driven outpainting to batch-process aspect ratio changes across multiple clips without manual frame-by-frame editing.

use_cases.title: Ad Creative Adaptation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams adapt hero ad creatives from square Instagram format to wide brand banners without scheduling reshoots or losing campaign momentum.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Aspect Ratio Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video outpainting skill to change my vertical 9:16 video to horizontal 16:9 format. The video is at [video_url].
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user converting a single video between common social media formats

prompt_templates.title: Directional Canvas Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas of my video at [video_url] by adding matching environment on the left and right sides. Preserve the original subject framing in the center. Maintain the same lighting and background style.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants precise control over which sides of the canvas get extended

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Pass Outpainting Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a two-pass outpainting pipeline on [video_url]. First pass extends the canvas 30 percent in all directions using Wan 2-7 edit-video. Then evaluate the output and report if seam quality is acceptable for the target platform.
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user running iterative outpainting with quality checkpoints

prompt_templates.title: Hero-Grade Quality Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess my video at [video_url] for hero ad delivery requirements. If the Wan 2-7 seam quality is insufficient for production use, direct me to the appropriate ComfyUI LTX 2-3 outpainting workflow with the specific parameters I need for spatial frame expansion.
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional user needing production-quality output with intelligent fallback routing

output_examples.input: Convert my vertical TikTok video to horizontal YouTube format
output_examples.output: I will run your video through Wan 2-7 edit-video with a prompt to extend the canvas to 16:9 horizontal. The model will add matching environment on the left and right sides while preserving your original subject and action in the center. Output will be saved to the out directory.

output_examples.input: I need hero ad quality for this brand video aspect ratio change
output_examples.output: For hero ad delivery with strict seam quality requirements, I recommend using the ComfyUI LTX 2-3 outpainting workflow instead of Wan 2-7. Open the dedicated spatial frame expansion workflow in the RunComfy ComfyUI cloud. This provides production-grade seam handling and motion-aware fill that Wan 2-7 cannot guarantee.

output_examples.input: Can I outpaint this old 4:3 footage to modern 16:9?
output_examples.output: Old footage outpainting needs careful seam handling beyond what prompt-shaped edit can deliver. I recommend the ComfyUI workflow path for this. Open the LTX 2-3 outpainting workflow in RunComfy for the best results with legacy aspect ratio material.

best_practices: Always include preservation language in prompts to prevent the model from restyling central content during canvas extension
Verify the runcomfy CLI is installed and authenticated before running outpainting commands
Use ComfyUI workflows instead of Wan 2-7 for production advertisement delivery where seam quality is critical
anti_patterns: Do not use Wan 2-7 edit-video for hero ad delivery with strict seam quality requirements, use ComfyUI workflows instead
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell, use verified npm packages only
Do not run more than two consecutive outpainting passes as visual quality degrades significantly beyond that

faq.question: What video formats does outpainting support?
faq.answer: The skill supports any video format accepted by the RunComfy Wan 2-7 edit-video model. Common formats like MP4 work well. The CLI handles format conversion automatically during processing.

faq.question: Can I outpaint videos without installing anything?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI installed. Use npm install -g @runcomfy/cli for a permanent installation or npx -y @runcomfy/cli for one-time use. A RunComfy account with valid authentication is also required.

faq.question: How long does video outpainting take?
faq.answer: Processing time depends on video length and resolution. Most short social media clips complete within a few minutes. The CLI polls the RunComfy API automatically and downloads results when the job finishes.

faq.question: Is my video data secure during outpainting?
faq.answer: Videos are transmitted to RunComfy servers for processing. The CLI stores authentication tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with restricted file permissions. RunComfy collects no telemetry from CLI usage.

faq.question: What is the difference between outpainting and inpainting?
faq.answer: Outpainting extends the canvas beyond the original frame by generating new content around the edges. Inpainting modifies content within the existing frame using masks to define edit regions. This skill handles outpainting only.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for image outpainting instead of video?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed for video outpainting only. For still image canvas extension, use the separate image-outpainting skill which provides dedicated single-image outpainting workflows through RunComfy.
